page: Microsoft Azure F80als_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Falsv7 server family. It is powered by an AMD Zen 9V45 processor with an x86_64 architecture, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance features 80 dedicated vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. It is configured with 160.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This low-memory configuration is designed specifically for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints.

This server type does not include any local storage, though it is premium storage capable for external disk attachments. It does not feature any onboard GPUs, relying entirely on its high-frequency CPU cores for processing. There are zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses bundled with the instance.

In benchmark evaluations, the F80als_v7 demonstrates top-tier performance across multiple categories. Its single-core and multi-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests rank in the top 10%, supported by high memory bandwidth for both small and large block read-only operations. Passmark CPU tests, including encryption, physics, and database operations, also place the instance in the top 10%. For specialized tasks, it achieves top-tier throughput in static web serving, redis SET operations, and bzip3 decompression. Large language model inference benchmarks show top-tier prompt processing and text generation speeds for small and medium models, though text generation for larger 70B models drops to the top 25% tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the F80als_v7 offers high compute density per resource unit due to its 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, making it a viable option for CPU-bound tasks where paying for excess memory is inefficient. The primary tradeoff is the complete lack of local storage and GPU acceleration. This server is suited for high-performance computing, batch processing, web serving, CPU-based machine learning inference, and database operations that leverage external premium storage.
description: The Microsoft Azure F80als_v7 is an x86_64 compute-optimized virtual machine from the Falsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 80 dedicated AMD Zen 9V45 vCPUs clocked at 4.5 GHz, paired with 160.0 GB of RAM, establishing a 2.0 GB per core memory ratio. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, but is premium storage capable. Benchmark results place this server in the top 10% for single-core and multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations. It also delivers strong performance in static web serving and small-to-medium LLM inference. Given its high compute density and low memory overhead, the F80als_v7 is highly cost-efficient for CPU-bound workloads. It is best suited for compute-heavy applications, batch processing, web serving, and database workloads utilizing external storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F80als_v7 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server with 80 dedicated AMD Zen vCPUs at 4.5 GHz and 160 GB RAM, delivering top-tier CPU and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F80als_v7 is a compute-optimized server with 80 AMD Zen vCPUs, 160 GB RAM, and top-tier benchmark performance.
tagline: A high-frequency, compute-dense virtual machine designed for CPU-bound workloads requiring top-tier processing power and minimal memory overhead.

160.0 GB of system memory with a 2.0 GB per core ratio
